0.q: we identified these two proteins ... that act as an ATP-binding protein and a bifunctional protein ... and show high sequence identity with BusAA and BusAB ... the operon was consequently referred to as busATha.

1.q: BusRTha, a transcription regulator that represses the expression of busATha, was characterized, and the repression was abrogated under high salinity.

2.q: The regulator can directly bind to the promoter sequence of busATha, and the binding sites encompassed the -35 and -10 boxes, composed of palindromic 5′-AAA(T/G)TGAC(C/A)(G/A)T(C/A)C-3′.
